column2: As a developer, I want unit tests for all features running on CI/CD so that code correctness is automatically verified.
column3: Write unit tests for WP-1 to WP-5 and unique feature · Configure CI/CD pipeline to run on push
column6: HIGH - CI/CD misconfiguration could cause all tests to fail silently; pipeline setup is a dependency for S4-2 and S4-3 deliverables

column2: As a developer, I want to find and fix 5 bugs using a static analysis tool so that the final codebase is clean and maintainable.
column3: Set up ESLint/SonarQube · Run analysis · Fix 5 flagged bugs Document fixes in commits
column6: HIGH - SonarQube/ESLint setup may flag too many issues making it hard to isolate the required 5; fixes could introduce regressions if not carefully tested

column2: As a developer, I want all user stories to have 2 acceptance tests and new code to be peer reviewed so that the final product meets quality standards.
column3: Write 2 ATs per user story Link ATs to stories in GitHub Open PRs Complete peer reviews
column6: MEDIUM - Acceptance tests may be incomplete or not properly linked to user stories in GitHub; peer review bottleneck if not scheduled early enough before the due date

column2: As an user, I'd like to know how difficult to cook the recipe

-Add attribute difficulty to recipe
-Add difficulty display to recipes in UI

column2: As an user, I'd want to track the calories of each recipe

-Add attribute calorie to recipe
-Add calories display to recipes in UI
column6: LOW- Extra feature that won't impact app performance

column2: As an user, I want to create a weekly meal plan so that I can organize my meals in advance
column3: -Design weekly meal planner UI-Create meal plan class/model-Implement backend endpoint for meal plan creation
column6: HIGH - Core part of the weekly planner feature; blocks all other planner features

column2: As an user, I want to view my meals in a weekly grid so that I can see my full week at a glance
column3: -Design and implement weekly grid UI-Fetch and display assigned meals per day-Add responsive layout for grid view
column6: MEDIUM - Visual clarity affects usability; grid layout may need revision across screen sizes

column2: As an user, I want to assign recipes to a specific day and meal type so that my plan is organized by breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snack
column3: -Add meal type selector (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack)-Add day-of-week selector-Implement assignment logic and save to backend
column6: HIGH - Core functionality of the planner; incorrect assignment breaks the whole plan structure

column2: As an user, I want to edit or remove meals from my planner so that I can adjust my plan when my schedule changes
column3: -Add edit button to each meal slot-Add remove/delete button per meal entry-Implement update and delete backend endpoints
column6: MEDIUM - Failure to update correctly could leave stale data in the planner

column2: As an user, I want the planner to prevent me from assigning the same recipe twice in the same week so that my meal plan stays varied
column3: -Implement duplicate detection logic on assignment-Display warning message on duplicate attempt-Block submission if duplicate detected
column6: LOW - Edge case feature; can be revisited if core planner features take priority

-Add search bar to UI
-Implement search function
column6: MEDIUM - As the website is still in its beginnings, looking through a small list of recipes makes search less needed immediately.

-Implement filter functions for searching

column2: As an new user, I can register an account and log in securely so I can access the platform.
column3:
-Design registration UI (email, password)
-Create login form UI with proper form validation
 -Implement password validation rules
- Create backend endpoint for user registration
 -Add error handling (invalid login, duplicate account, weak password)
column6: HIGH - Core entry point; failure blocks all other features. Security vulnerabilities if passwords not properly encrypted.

 -Design form for allergy information (front end only)
